excerpt: Without the 'Lord,' the 'servant' (ʿabd) cannot exist. Every attribute from which obedience is absent belongs to lordship; every attribute hidden in lordship is manifest in servitude.

The pathway to liberation requires forbidding the self every acquisition it desires, and accustoming it to bear all that it abhors. The essence of this practice is that one abandon all rest, choose solitude, and pursue the knowledge of Allah.
If you lower your gaze, you will witness wonders.
Whoever knows his own self knows his Lord.
There is no path to Allah save through knowledge. Knowledge is man's adornment in this world, his guide to the Hereafter, and the means of attaining Allah's pleasure.
Among you, he who judges against someone with the least carefulness is most despicable to Allah.

Command people to what is right and forbid them from what is wrong, and bear patiently whatever befalls you, and be steadfast with them. But when you see people pursuing evil, selfish desires, and arrogance, then turn away from them.
Most despicable of all creatures is one known for knowledge alone but not for deeds.

True gratitude is expressed when a person sincerely seeks Allah's forgiveness for his incapacity to be grateful for even one of Allah's blessings. Therefore it becomes incumbent upon him to recite Allah's glorification (tasbīḥ), praise (taḥmīd), and thanksgiving (tahmīd).
One of his companions came to his dwelling and asked his family where Abū Dharr had gone. They replied: 'He has gone out.'
O Allah, I cannot render You the praise You deserve, for You praise Yourself more perfectly than any praise I can give.

Satan gains dominion over Allah's servants only when they have abandoned His remembrance, grown disdainful when faced with His prohibition, felt complacent in their disobedience, and forgotten that Allah witnesses their secret acts.

He who is permitted to earn, though his body labors, keeps his heart in trust toward Allah. Should he accumulate much wealth, he is as a trustee who knows that having or lacking wealth are equal. If he withholds, he withholds for Allah's sake; if he spends, he spends according to Allah's command.
source_authority: Ismaili teaching on trust and provision

excerpt: Not a day passes but the sea asks permission of its Lord to drown the children of Adam. The earth asks permission to swallow them. The angels ask permission to hasten their punishment and prolong their torment.
source_authority: Divine speech cited in Ismaili tradition

excerpt: The angels ask: 'Why do You withhold from him?' Allah replies: 'I am awaiting his repentance.'
source_authority: Divine discourse cited in Ismaili tradition

excerpt: Whether he comes to Me repenting by day or by night, I shall accept his repentance. I shall not hear from him any word of complaint or reproach.
source_authority: Divine speech cited in Ismaili tradition

excerpt: Whoever draws near to Me, I advance to meet him from afar; whoever turns away from Me, I call out to him from near.
source_authority: Divine speech cited in Ismaili tradition

excerpt: Have you found any lord but Me? With Me, a good deed is recorded as ten of its like, and I increase beyond this. A sin is recorded as one of its like, and I forgive whenever I will.
source_authority: Divine speech cited in Ismaili tradition
